key_insight: The paper introduces a physics-informed DeepONet surrogate that predicts linear elastic displacement fields directly from geometry and boundary conditions, enabling fast fracture-aware inference without FEM training data.
engineering_relevance: This could support real-time structural health monitoring by providing a physics-consistent displacement predictor for fractured elastic components.
method: Encode fracture geometry with a dedicated representation for DeepONet input.
Condition the model on boundary conditions to predict displacement fields.
Enforce traction-free fracture boundaries using a localized weak penalty term.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a numerical example on one representative fracture geometry, showing feasibility of the formulation rather than a broad benchmark evaluation.
watch_item: Performance is only demonstrated on a single fracture geometry, so generalization to diverse crack shapes and loading cases remains to be validated.
paper_title: Learning Physics-Informed Surrogate Model of Linear Elastic Displacement Fields from Geometry
